add_node_subtitle: Register a new node to your account.
add_node_limit: Free tier allows 3 nodes. Upgrade for more.
landing_hero_sub1: Type "why is pve-storage slow?" and get a plain-English answer.
landing_steps_sub: From zero to full homelab intelligence in one terminal session.
landing_step1_desc: One command installs a lightweight agent on each node. No inbound ports to open, under 50MB RAM at runtime.
landing_step2_desc: CPU, memory, disk, network, Docker containers, services. Outbound only — your firewall stays closed.
landing_step3_desc: Type questions in plain English. Get smart alerts, root cause analysis, and daily intelligence digests with per-node grades.
landing_features_sub: labwatch understands your homelab, not just its metrics.
landing_feat_nlq_desc: Type "why is pve-storage slow?" and get a diagnostic answer. Not a graph — an explanation with root cause analysis.
landing_feat_alerts_desc: Deduplication, auto-resolution, and pattern detection. Catches slow disk fills and memory leaks before they become outages.
landing_feat_fleet_desc: Daily plain-English digest of your entire lab. Per-node letter grades, trends, anomalies, and actionable recommendations.
landing_feat_docker_desc: Container health, restart loop detection, CPU and memory per container. Sees inside your stacks, not just the host.
landing_feat_stack_desc: Runs alongside Grafana, Uptime Kuma, Prometheus. labwatch adds the intelligence layer they don't have.
landing_feat_oss_desc: MIT-licensed Go agent you can audit. Outbound-only connections. Self-host the server or use our hosted version. Your data stays yours.
landing_demo_sub: Real queries, real answers. No graphs to interpret.
landing_install_sub: Works on Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch, Alpine, and NixOS.
landing_install_note: Or download the binary directly from
landing_install_note2: MIT-licensed. Agent source on GitHub (coming soon).
landing_pricing_sub: Start free. Scale when you need to.
landing_price_free_desc: Everything, capped at 3 nodes. No credit card.
landing_price_starter_desc: For small homelabs with a handful of nodes.
landing_price_pro_desc: For serious labs — unlimited nodes and priority.
landing_price_selfhost_desc: Run the server on your own hardware. Full control.
login_subtitle: Sign in with your email or API token.
login_token_hint: This is the 64-character token you received when you signed up. You can also find it in /etc/labwatch/config.yaml on your node.
setpw_subtitle: Set a password for your account so you can log in with email.
signup_subtitle: Free tier — no credit card required.
signup_email_hint: We'll send your credentials here. No spam.
signup_password_hint: Used to log in to your dashboard. You can also log in with your API token.
signup_hostname_hint: Display name for your first node. You can rename it later.
signup_free_note: Free tier includes 3 nodes, 24h metric retention, and basic alerts.
signup_step4_note: Metrics will appear within 60 seconds of starting the agent.
dashboard_upgrade_checkout_failed: Could not start checkout. Please try again in a moment.
dashboard_widget_library_subtitle: add more widgets to your dashboard
dashboard_demo_banner: This is a demo with sample data.
dashboard_ask_demo_placeholder: Try asking about the demo lab...
dashboard_no_labs_desc: Install the agent on any Linux machine to start collecting metrics and intelligence.
dashboard_query_cpu: which node uses the most cpu?
dashboard_query_disk: how much disk space do I have?
lab_admin_delete_desc: Permanently remove this lab and all its data. This cannot be undone.
lab_admin_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this lab? This cannot be undone.
support_subtitle: Need help with labwatch? We're here for you.
support_email_desc: Send us an email and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.
support_docs_desc: Browse our docs for setup guides, troubleshooting, and API reference.
support_github_desc: Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on GitHub.
support_faq_q1: How do I find my API token?
support_faq_a1: Your API token is in /etc/labwatch/config.yaml on any node where the agent is installed. You can also find it in your dashboard under Administration.
support_faq_q2: The agent isn't sending metrics. What do I do?
support_faq_a2: Check that the agent is running with 'systemctl status labwatch'. Verify your config.yaml has the correct server_url and api_token. Check firewall rules allow outbound HTTPS.
support_faq_q3: Can I self-host the labwatch server?
support_faq_a3: Yes! Self-hosting is a first-class path. See /self-hosted for the full docker-compose quickstart, environment variables, and agent config — everything is MIT licensed on GitHub.
dashboard_edit_hint: drag to reorder · × to remove · + add widget at bottom
landing_price_business_desc: For teams running 50+ servers who need SLA-backed support and custom integrations.
